'''Herios IV''' was a [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located within the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Herios sector/Legends|Herios sector]]. At some point during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a group of [[Slaver/Legends|slavers]] brought the [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] [[Baraduk]] to the planet's [[Unidentified Imperial colony|Imperial colony]], but the Barabel escaped into the jungle, stealing several weapons from the settlement. After a prolonged hunt for Baraduk failed to result in his capture, the colony's [[Unidentified Imperial governor (Herios IV)|governor]] granted the Barabel amnesty in return for the fugitive's departure from the planet.

==Description==
Herios IV was a [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that occupied the fourth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="GG10" /> within the [[Herios system/Legends|Herios system]], a part of the [[Herios sector/Legends|Herios sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="Slice" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==History==
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a conflict between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], a group of [[Slaver/Legends|slavers]] arrived on Herios IV, bringing with them the [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] [[Baraduk]], whom they had previously captured. Upon their arrival at the Imperial colony on the planet, Baraduk managed to break free from his captors. The Barabel raided the settlement's arms depot, stealing several weapons, including a portable [[Missile launcher/Legends|missile launcher]], and escaped into the jungle.<ref name="GG10" />

The colony's [[Unidentified Imperial governor (Herios IV)|Imperial governor]] vowed to recapture Baraduk, but the six-[[Standard month/Legends|month]]-long hunt that followed was a failure, resulting in the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of fifteen [[Imperial Army trooper/Legends|Imperial Army soldiers]] while the Barabel fugitive remained at large. Eventually, the [[Governor/Legends|governor]] offered Baraduk a quiet amnesty, asking in exchange that the Barabel leave Herios IV and never return. Baraduk accepted the offer and left the planet.<ref name="GG10" />

==Inhabitants==
Herios IV was the site of an Imperial colony overseen by a governor during the Galactic Civil War. The settlement included an arms depot.<ref name="GG10" />

==Behind the scenes==
Herios IV was mentioned in the [[1994]] ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' supplement ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'', which was written by [[Rick D. Stuart]] for [[West End Games]].<ref name="GG10" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Herios [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet, in grid square T-16.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>
